title: struthiopteris eburnea
intro: Struthiopteris eburnea, also known as the ivory fern, is a type of ancient fossil fern that was once found in various parts of the world, including Europe and North America. Although it is no longer found in its original form, extracts from this plant have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Struthiopteris eburnea include its ability to aid in the treatment of wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and promote healing. In herbal preparations, Struthiopteris eburnea is often used in the form of tinctures, teas, and creams, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human use.
